LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE COURT RULES UPON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE AS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS THE SAME FACTS AND DATES FOR THE FRAUD ARE ALLEGED IN BOTH THE ORIGINAL AND AMENDED COMPLAINTS. THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE NOVEMBER 18, 2003 LETTER SENT BY REGAN ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF KNEW OF DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FRAUD AT THAT TIME. THE LETTER STATES THAT DEFENDANT "MATERIALLY MISREPRESENTED" CLAIMS WITH RESPECT TO ITS CLIENT, SELECT. (SEE HANSON DECLARATION.) THE LETTER IS UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ON NOTICE AND A "REASONABLE PERSON" WOULD HAVE KNOWN TO INVESTIGATE FURTHER. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STATUTORY PENALTY WHICH IS ALSO TIME-BARRED. THE NOVEMBER 18, 2003 LETTER IS THE CRUX OF THE RUNNING OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. ALSO, ALL OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT CONSIDERED ALL EVIDENCE. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

RACHEL S. PRESSMAN, ESQ., ((213)485-1234, FOR Defendant/MOVING PARTY, Select Personnel Service, Inc. (TELEPHONIC APPEARANCE)
SASCHA HENRY, ESQ., (213)617-5562, FOR STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND

HEARING WAS HAD. RULING AFTER HEARING: Defendant Select Personnel Service, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And The Production Of Deposition Testimony From Plaintiff. Granted in part, moot in part, and denied in part. The motion is moot as to Request For Production No. 1. The motion as to Nos. 4 and 8 is granted, limited to payment information, including loss runs and reports on payment in each claim. The court does not find good cause for production of medical information at this time. The motion is granted as to Nos. 11, 12, and 14, limited to negotiations, drafts, the auditing process, and the underwriting process.
The motion is moot as to Usha Prasad. As to Cathy Cockcayne, her medical condition is such that she is not compelled to submit to deposition at this time. If she is unable to be deposed prior to May 21, 2010, State Compensation Fund must bring a motion for Protective Order. Sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported. (610/EAH)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ONVOI BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Sascha Henry, Esq. of Sheppard Mullin (213) 617-5562 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Drew E. Pomerance, Esq. & Edward Donohue, Esq. of Roxborough Pomerance Nye & Adreani (818) 992-9999, appearing for Defendant and moving party; Jacob A. Regenstreif, of Latham & Watkins (213) 891-7572 Appearing thru CourtCall, for Defendant Select Personal Services. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. THE COURT RULES UPON THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE AS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS THE SAME FACTS AND DATES FOR THE FRAUD ARE ALLEGED IN BOTH THE ORIGINAL AND AMENDED COMPLAINTS. THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE NOVEMBER 18, 2003 LETTER SENT BY REGAN ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF KNEW OF DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FRAUD AT THAT TIME. THE LETTER STATES THAT DEFENDANT "MATERIALLY MISREPRESENTED" CLAIMS WITH RESPECT TO ITS CLIENT, SELECT. (SEE HANSON DECLARATION.) THE LETTER IS UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ON NOTICE AND A "REASONABLE PERSON" WOULD HAVE KNOWN TO INVESTIGATE FURTHER. SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED AS TO 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR STATUTORY PENALTY WHICH IS ALSO TIME-BARRED. The November 18, 2003 letter is the crux of the running of the statute of limitations. Also, all objections to evidence are overruled. The Court considered all evidence. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
